description: Deduplication system detecting a duplicate Identity.API connection refused incident (video-based submission)
scenario: Second report of the same Identity.API container crash — this time submitted with a video recording (error_recording.mp4)

original_incident.title: Identity service down — SocketException Connection refused on port 5105

duplicate_report.title: Cannot log in — video shows socket error on identity service
duplicate_report.description: I recorded the error in a video. The browser shows a connection error and nothing loads after login. Seems like the identity container is gone.

multimodal_analysis.gemini_video_observation: Video shows browser DevTools Network tab with multiple failed requests to localhost:5105/connect/token returning ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED. Login form is visible but unresponsive. Terminal in corner shows 'docker ps' output with identity-api container in 'Exited (1)' state.
multimodal_analysis.signal_extracted: SocketException: Connection refused → localhost:5105 (Identity.API). Container exited. Login completely broken.

triage_result.reasoning: Video evidence shows identical symptoms: SocketException Connection refused on port 5105 (Identity.API), same production environment, same time window as SRE-1003. Root cause (Identity container OOMKilled) matches exactly. Gemini multimodal analysis of the video confirms the same connection refusal pattern visible in the error log and screenshot. Marking as duplicate to prevent noise.

action: Incident marked as DUPLICATE. No new ticket created. Original ticket SRE-1003 referenced.
benefit: Deduplication prevented 1 redundant P1 ticket and 2 redundant PagerDuty/Slack alerts for the same active incident.
multimodal_value: Even though this report came as a video (no text log), Gemini's multimodal analysis correctly extracted the service name, port, and error type from the video frames — enabling accurate deduplication against an existing text-log-based incident.
